title: Truth, Tools, and AI as Amplifier
summary: Learn how to verify practical claims, use AI to compare sources, and record decisions with citations before acting.
plain_language: This module teaches you how to check whether information is real, current, and useful before you act on it.

title: Measure, Map, and Inventory
summary: Convert vague household conditions into measured quantities, system maps, and a resilience inventory you can build on.
plain_language: This module helps you see what your household depends on before something fails.

title: Water I: Storage and Treatment
summary: Plan potable water quantities, storage intervals, and treatment choices using a household-specific decision process.
plain_language: This module helps you decide how much water to store and how to make questionable water safer to use.

title: Water II: Testing and Household Planning
summary: Use simple household testing and planning steps to turn water storage and treatment choices into a 72-hour and 2-week plan.
plain_language: This module turns water ideas into a real plan you can check, update, and explain.

title: Food I: Pantry Design, Rotation, and Preservation Boundaries
summary: Design a pantry around calories, shelf life, budget, and storage conditions, then add a clear rotation and preservation safety boundary.
plain_language: This module helps you build food resilience without turning your pantry into clutter or guessing about preservation safety.

title: Food II: Seed Starting and Garden Planning
summary: Turn gardening from aspiration into a small, reliable production system using timing, setup, and a simple crop plan.
plain_language: This module helps you start growing food in a way that is small enough to sustain and structured enough to learn from.

title: Shelter I: Building Envelope and Weatherization
summary: Learn where homes lose heat and comfort, then audit drafts, leakage, and weatherization priorities room by room.
plain_language: This module helps you understand how air, heat, and moisture move through a building so you can improve comfort and efficiency.

title: Repair I: Moisture, Tools, Shutoffs, and Patching
summary: Combine moisture control, hand-tool literacy, and simple shutoff and sealing actions so small problems stop becoming bigger ones.
plain_language: This module helps you act early on leaks, dampness, and small failures instead of waiting until they become expensive.

title: Power I: Circuit Basics and Device Label Reading
summary: Build electrical literacy around volts, amps, watts, watt-hours, circuit limits, and what device labels actually tell you.
plain_language: This module gives you enough electrical literacy to read labels, compare loads, and avoid obvious planning mistakes.

title: Power II: Critical Loads and Runtime
summary: Turn electrical literacy into an outage load audit and realistic runtime estimates based on actual priorities and assumptions.
plain_language: This module helps you decide what must stay on during an outage and how long different power sources could support it.

title: Power III and Community Layer: Backup Planning and Local Resource Mapping
summary: Produce tiered backup power options and connect them to a local map of skills, tools, places, and shared assets.
plain_language: This module helps you compare backup options realistically and then locate the people and resources nearby that increase resilience.

title: Capstone: Teach Forward and Archive the Capability
summary: Package one completed capability into a short, usable lesson with a durable handout so the knowledge can move beyond the individual learner.
plain_language: This module turns private learning into public usefulness by asking you to teach one thing clearly and leave behind something others can reuse.
